title: Pyrus-cydonia cydonia lusitanica
taxonomy: Pyrus-cydonia cydonia lusitanica is a variety of the quince tree, specifically classified as a subspecies of Pyrus cydonia. It belongs to the family Rosaceae, which includes other fruit-bearing trees like apples and pears. The genus Pyrus is further classified under the order Rosales, comprising plants with showy flowers and simple leaves. At the kingdom level, Pyrus-cydonia cydonia lusitanica is classified as Plantae, encompassing all plant life. This classification reflects the quince tree's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plant species.

intro: Pyrus cydonia Lusitanica, also known as Portuguese quince, is a subspecies of the quince plant (Pyrus cydonia) that is native to Portugal and is characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible fruit. The health benefits of Pyrus cydonia Lusitanica include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against various diseases and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus cydonia Lusitanica include teas, infusions, and jams, which are used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation.
